title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. subalpina
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. subalpina, also known as the alpine columbine, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, soothing coughs, and promoting relaxation. Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. subalpina can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
